steps.title: Use the diagonal triangle
steps.content: A rectangle's diagonal and side lengths form a right triangle. Let $b$ be the longer side, with hypotenuse $4\sqrt{17}$ and shorter leg $4$.

steps.title: Solve for the side
steps.content: Square and subtract. $$16+b^2=16\cdot17$$ $$16+b^2=272$$ $$b^2=256$$ $$b=16$$ The answer is 16.
